content_en: This type of envy goes against the spirit of brotherhood in Islam. • Sultan points out that this hadith demonstrates that the Companions put into the practice what they learned from the Prophet (peace be upon him). When they heard about good deeds such as charity, they gave in charity. However, some of them did not have the means to do so. So they brought that matter to the attention of the Prophet (peace be upon him), saying that they were not able to perform those deeds as the people of wealth were able.1 • The one who orders good and eradicates evil should recall that his deed is first and foremost an act of charity for others. His intention should be to better others. He should not have the intention of commanding others or putting himself in authority over others. He is doing an act of goodness toward others and this point he should always keep in rnind.2 • If they are done with the proper intention, permissible deeds can become rewarded acts that take one closer to Allah. • This hadith demonstrates that it is permissible to ask a scholar the reasoning for his statement. In this hadith, the Companions questioned the Prophet (peace be upon him) as to how fulfilling one's desires could lead one to being rewarded from Allah. The Prophet (peace be upon him) did not object to their request for an explanation. Instead, he showed them the evidence or the reasoning behind his statement. 3 • Sultan also points out that presenting one's evidence for an opinion makes it easier for the people to accept and believe in one's statement. Therefore, the scholars should be willing to give their evidence when questioned and should not feel that this is an insult to their status.4 • Al-Haitami points out that this hadith demonstrates that the rich person who is thankful to Allah and gives out of his excess wealth is in a superior position to the poor person who is patient, all other things being equal. The Prophet (peace be upon him) showed the poor Companions deeds that they could perform to bring them close in reward to the richer Companions. However, when the richer Companions performed the same deeds, the 1 Sultaan, p. 224. 2 Cf., ibn Uthaimeen, Sharh Riyaadh, vol. 3, p. 1 98. 3 Cf., ibn Daqeeq al-Eid, Sharh, p. 69. 4 Cf., Sultaan, p. 228; al-Bugha and Mistu, p. 1 83. Hadith #25: Acts of Charity Prophet (peace be upon him) had to explain to the poorer Companions that such was Allah's grace that He bestows upon whom He wills. He further states, as ibn Daqeeq al-Eid stated, that if a poor person and rich person are equal in performing the obligatory deeds but the rich person gives in charity while the poor person simply makes more remembrance of Allah, the place of the rich person will be superior as his voluntary deeds benefit others. Some argue that being poor is better because the person develops patience and purity. Al-Haitami counters that by saying that a rich person also experiences thankfulness to Allah as well as purifying himself from greed, lusts, boasting and so forth. Al-Haitami closes his argument by saying that the Prophet (peace be upon him) began as a poor and patient person but he ended his mission rich (able to fulfill his needs) and thankful. In general, Allah brings the life of the messengers and prophets to an end while they are in their best state of worship and closeness to Allah. This is a further sign that the place of a rich person who is thankful and giving is better than being poor person who is patient, all other things held equal. He also states that the poor person will get the reward for patience as well as for his intention to do good if he had wealth. However, the rich person will be rewarded for his actual acts of charity and his thankfulness. The intention of a poor person cannot be considered something definitive while the acts of the rich person are definitive. In other words, although a poor person may claim-- even to himselfthat if he had wealth he would spend it in a proper way, only Allah knows for certain if that intention is completely true or not. 1 The important point that one can conclude from al-Haitami's discussion is that, from an Islamic perspective, there is no virtue necessarily in being poor and there is no sin in being wealthy. Indeed, being wealthy can be a much more virtuous position. It is good for Muslims to make money so that they may support themselves, their families and give the excess in charity for the sake of Allah. Allah knows best. • In many societies there is friction or hatred between the rich or upper classes and the poor or lower classes. Taataay points out that Islam has removed all such hatred and friction from society.
content_id:
Jenis iri hati ini bertentangan dengan semangat persaudaraan dalam Islam.

Sulthan menunjukkan bahwa hadis ini menunjukkan bahwa para sahabat mempraktikkan apa yang mereka pelajari dari Nabi ﷺ. Ketika mereka mendengar tentang amal-amal kebaikan seperti sedekah, mereka bersedekah. Namun, sebagian dari mereka tidak memiliki kemampuan untuk melakukannya. Jadi mereka membawa masalah itu kepada perhatian Nabi ﷺ, mengatakan bahwa mereka tidak mampu melakukan amal-amal tersebut sebagaimana orang-orang berharta mampu melakukannya.

Orang yang memerintahkan kebaikan dan memberantas kemungkaran harus ingat bahwa amalnya pertama dan utamanya adalah tindakan sedekah bagi orang lain. Niatnya harus memperbaiki orang lain. Beliau tidak boleh berniat untuk memerintahi orang lain atau menempatkan dirinya dalam posisi berkuasa atas orang lain. Beliau sedang melakukan tindakan kebaikan terhadap orang lain dan poin ini harus selalu beliau ingat.

Jika dilakukan dengan niat yang benar, tindakan-tindakan yang diperbolehkan dapat menjadi tindakan yang mendapat pahala yang mendekatkan seseorang kepada Allah. Hadis ini menunjukkan bahwa diperbolehkan untuk meminta penjelasan kepada seorang ulama atas pernyataannya. Dalam hadis ini, para sahabat mempertanyakan kepada Nabi ﷺ bagaimana memenuhi keinginan seseorang dapat mengarah pada mendapatkan pahala dari Allah. Nabi ﷺ tidak menolak permintaan penjelasan mereka. Sebaliknya, beliau menunjukkan kepada mereka bukti atau alasan di balik pernyataannya.

Sulthan juga menunjukkan bahwa menyajikan bukti seseorang untuk suatu pendapat membuatnya lebih mudah bagi orang-orang untuk menerima dan mempercayai pernyataannya. Oleh karena itu, para ulama harus bersedia memberikan bukti mereka ketika ditanya dan tidak boleh merasa bahwa ini adalah penghinaan terhadap status mereka.

Al-Haitami menunjukkan bahwa hadis ini menunjukkan bahwa orang kaya yang bersyukur kepada Allah dan memberikan dari kelebihan hartanya berada dalam posisi yang lebih unggul dari orang miskin yang bersabar, dengan segala hal lain yang setara. Nabi ﷺ menunjukkan kepada para sahabat yang miskin amal-amal yang dapat mereka lakukan untuk mendekatkan pahala mereka kepada para sahabat yang lebih kaya. Namun, ketika para sahabat yang lebih kaya melakukan amal-amal yang sama itu pula, Nabi ﷺ harus menjelaskan kepada para sahabat yang lebih miskin bahwa itulah karunia Allah yang Dia anugerahkan kepada siapa yang Dia kehendaki.

Al-Haitami menutup argumennya dengan mengatakan bahwa Nabi ﷺ memulai sebagai orang miskin yang sabar tetapi beliau mengakhiri misinya dalam keadaan kaya (mampu memenuhi kebutuhannya) dan bersyukur. Secara umum, Allah mengakhiri kehidupan para rasul dan nabi sementara mereka berada dalam keadaan ibadah dan kedekatan kepada Allah yang terbaik. Ini adalah tanda lebih lanjut bahwa kedudukan orang kaya yang bersyukur dan dermawan lebih baik dari orang miskin yang bersabar, dengan segala hal lain yang setara.

Poin penting yang dapat disimpulkan dari pembahasan Al-Haitami adalah bahwa, dari perspektif Islam, tidak ada keutamaan yang melekat dalam kemiskinan dan tidak ada dosa dalam kekayaan. Bahkan, menjadi kaya dapat menjadi posisi yang jauh lebih utama. Adalah baik bagi kaum Muslim untuk menghasilkan uang agar mereka dapat menghidupi diri mereka sendiri, keluarga mereka, dan memberikan kelebihan dalam sedekah demi ridha Allah. Wallahu a'lam.

Di banyak masyarakat terdapat gesekan atau kebencian antara orang-orang kaya atau kelas atas dan orang-orang miskin atau kelas bawah. Taatay menunjukkan bahwa Islam telah menghilangkan semua kebencian dan gesekan seperti itu dari masyarakat.
content_ar: This type of الحسد goes against the الروح of brotherhood in الإسلام. • Sultan points out that this الحديث demonstrates that the الصحابة put into the practice what they learned from النبي ﷺ (ﷺ). عندما they heard about الخير deeds مثل الصدقة, they gave in الصدقة. غير أن, some of them did not have the means to do لذا. لذا they brought that matter to the attention of النبي ﷺ (ﷺ), saying that they were not able to perform those deeds as the people of الثروة were able.1 • The one who orders الخير and eradicates الشر should recall that his deed is أولاً and foremost an act of الصدقة for others. His intention should be to better others. He should not have the intention of commanding others or putting himself in authority over others. He is doing an act of goodness toward others and this point he should always keep in rnind.2 • إذا they are done with the proper intention, permissible deeds can become rewarded acts that take one closer to الله. • This الحديث demonstrates that يجوز to ask a scholar the reasoning for his statement. In this الحديث, the الصحابة questioned النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) as to how fulfilling one's desires could lead one to being rewarded from الله. النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) did not object to their request for an explanation. Instead, he showed them the evidence or the reasoning behind his statement. 3 • Sultan أيضاً points out that presenting one's evidence for an opinion makes it easier for the people to accept and believe in one's statement. لذلك, the العلماء should be willing to give their evidence عندما questioned and should not feel that this is an insult to their status.4 • Al-Haitami points out that this الحديث demonstrates that the rich person who is thankful to الله and gives out of his excess الثروة is in a superior position to the poor person who is patient, all other things being equal. النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) showed the poor الصحابة deeds that they could perform to bring them close in الأجر to the richer الصحابة. غير أن, عندما the richer الصحابة performed the same deeds, the 1 Sultaan, p. 224. 2 Cf., ibn Uthaimeen, Sharh Riyaadh, vol. 3, p. 1 98. 3 Cf., ibn Daqeeq al-Eid, Sharh, p. 69. 4 Cf., Sultaan, p. 228; al-Bugha and Mistu, p. 1 83. الحديث #25: Acts of الصدقة النبي (ﷺ) had to explain to the poorer الصحابة that such was الله's الفضل that He bestows upon whom He wills. He further states, as ibn Daqeeq al-Eid stated, that إذا a poor person and rich person are equal in performing the obligatory deeds but the rich person gives in الصدقة بينما the poor person simply makes more الذكر of الله, the place of the rich person will be superior as his voluntary deeds benefit others. Some argue that being poor is better لأن the person develops الصبر and purity. Al-Haitami counters that by saying that a rich person أيضاً experiences thankfulness to الله as well as purifying himself from الطمع, lust
